CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-19-2014 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-10-2014 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jun-03-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Michael Alfaro'S, Macada Inc.'S, Titan Management Group Llc'S, And Aztech Maintenance/Development Llc'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Dec-17-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ROGER MEREDITH, ESQ., 415.616.5959, FOR PLAINTIFF CAROL ALFARO.
NATALIE VANCE, ESQ,. AND DARRELL SPENCE, ESQ., 916.444.7573, FOR DEFENDANTS MICHAEL ALFARO, MACADA INC., TITAN MANAGEMENT GROUP LLC AND AZTECH MAINTENANCE/DEVELOPMENT LLC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendants Michael Alfaro's, Macada Inc.'s, Titan Management Group LLC'S, And Aztech Maintenance/Development LLC'S Motion For Summary Adjudication is Granted as to Issue nos. 1 ' 4, denied as to Issue nos. 6 ' 10. The request for judicial notice is granted. For the purposes of this motion only, Defendants assume that Plaintiff is a shareholder of Macada, Inc.. (P&A, p. 1:27-28, fn. 1.) For Issue no. 1, there are no triable issues of fact that Defendant Alfaro did not affirmatively make the misrepresentations alleged in the Complaint. (Complaint, 62; UMF nos. 5, 6.) To the extent that Plaintiff relies on a subsequent verbal agreement made outside formation of the corporation, these facts fall outside of the pleadings. (FPI Development, Inc. v. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 380. See AMF nos. 14 ' 16.) For Issue no. 2, the fraud claim is time-barred. The doctrines of continuing violation, continuous accrual and equitable estoppel do not toll the statute of limitations in the instant action. (CCP 338(d); Doe v. Roman Catholic Bishop of Sacramento (2010) 189 Cal.App.4th 1423, 1430.) Plaintiff has not pled tolling of the statute of limitations in the Complaint, and it falls outside the pleadings. It is undisputed that Plaintiff discovered the alleged fraud in 2001 and even provided Defendant Alfaro with semi-monthly statements of the amounts that he purportedly owed. (UMF nos. 11, 12; AMF no. 17.) For Issue no. 3, the fraud claims are not actionable against Defendants Aztech and Titan. Plaintiff's fraud claim is based on the misrepresentations made in 1999 that predate the formation of Defendants Aztech and Titan, and thus, Defendant Alfaro was not acting as their agent when the misrepresentations were made. (See Ach v. Finkelstein (1968) 264 Cal.App.2d 667, 677.) For Issue no. 4, both parties agree that the conspiracy claim is predicated on the fraud claims. For Issue no. 5, Corporations Code 309 provides a civil right of action. (Corp. Code 309(c).) Defendants' reliance on Berg & Berg Enterprises, LLC v. Boyle (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 1020, 1041, Lehman v. Superior Court (2006) 145 Cal.App.4th 109, 123; Briano v. Rubio (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 1167 are not dispositive as the Court did not address the viability of a cause of action under Corporations Code 309. For Issue no. 6 ' 10, as the only other shareholder in a closely held corporation and Defendant Alfaro's withdrawal of monies to the benefit of himself and to the detriment of Plaintiff in violation of their profit sharing agreement, Plaintiff may assert her claims as an individual, rather than a derivative action, against Defendant Alfaro. Jara v. Suprema Meats, Inc. (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 1238, 1257-58. See Friedman, Cal. Practice Guide: Corp. (Rutter) 6:598.2.) Order signed in open court. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Defendants' motion to set aside Court Trial/Prove-up judgment.
Dept. 514, Judge: Donald J. Sullivan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Sonia Wong #11288.

Jeff Cluett, Esq. appeared for the Defenants/Moving Parties Michael Alfaro et al.
Roger Meredith, Esq. for Plaintiff Carol Alfaro.

After hearing arguments and considering of the moving papers and oppositions by counsel, the court granted defendants' moiton. With pending payment of attorney fees and costs in the amount of $3,426.00 by the defendant to Mr. Meredith's office on or before 4 p.m. of 09-28-12. New trial date to be set after payment was made and stipulated by counsel or the court will set a new trial date.

MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Court Trial/Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Donald J. Sullivan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Sonia Wong #11288.

Roger Meredith, Esq. for and with the Plaintiff: Carol Alfaro, was placed under oath and direct examined.
No appearance by Defendants Michael Alfaro, Macada Inc., Titan Management Group, LLC., Aztech Maintenance/Development, LLC.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff and against defendant for Judgment as follows:

Loss of Income - $144,000.00
Attorney Fees - $34,841.00
Costs - $10,9852.50
Cash out of 50% worth Shareholders of the corporation - $1,500,000.00 for a Total of $1,689,793.50
